title: Views 🖼️
description: Virtual tables that save you typing.

slides.title: The Daily Grind ☕
slides.content:
# Brutal Repetition

Imagine that every single day, your boss asks you for a report on "Active Premium Users in Europe". 

To get this, you have to write a massive 20-line `JOIN` query connecting 5 different tables. Doing this manually every day is utterly exhausting.

slides.title: Virtual Tables 🖼️
slides.content:
## CREATE VIEW

You can save that exact 20-line query as a **View**.

```sql
CREATE VIEW european_premium_users AS
SELECT ... (your 20 line query here);
```

Now, tomorrow, all you have to type is:
`SELECT * FROM european_premium_users;`

The database treats the View exactly like a real table!

quizData.question: What fundamentally is a View in SQL?

options.text: A physical backup duplicate of your entire database
A saved, named SQL query that dynamically acts exactly like a 'virtual table' when you SELECT from it

quizData.explanation: Views don't actually store data. They dynamically run the underlying saved query every time you call them.

slides.title: Security Filters 🛡️
slides.content:
## Hiding Data with Views

Views are amazing for security. Imagine you have a `users` table with `passwords` and `credit_cards`.

You can create a View called `public_user_info` that ONLY selects `name` and `email`. Then, you give your marketing team access to the *View*, but block them from the real table! They can never see the passwords.

quizData.question: How can Views significantly improve database security?

options.text: They allow administrators to precisely restrict which columns and rows certain users are allowed to see by abstracting the real tables behind a safe, limited virtual table

quizData.explanation: Views restrict access seamlessly. Users interact with the clean View, completely blind to the sensitive data hidden in the base tables.
